<!--
RFC issue-body template. An RFC is a CONVERSATION: use it when the boundary
crosses repos, teams, or external consumers and you are requesting comment
BEFORE deciding (precedent: RFC-001 #543, the Personalization service
event-emission boundary). If the decision is this repo's alone, skip straight
to an ADR. An accepted RFC usually concludes in an ADR that records the
decision permanently.

Issue title: `RFC-NNN: <title>` (next NNN from the issue search).
Labels: P-tier + affected agent labels. Comment-by date is a commitment —
silence past it is consent to the recommendation.
-->

# RFC-NNN: <title>

**Status**: Comment requested · **Comment by**: <YYYY-MM-DD>
**Deciders**: <owner + the people whose systems this touches>
**Boundary**: <which repos / services / teams this crosses>

## Summary

<!-- Three sentences: the situation, the proposal, what feedback is needed. -->

## Context

<!-- Why now. Link the PRD/Goal/incident that raised this. State what is
already fixed (Locks from upstream specs) vs genuinely open. -->

## Proposal

<!-- The recommended design at the boundary: contracts, ownership, sequencing.
Concrete enough to disagree with — wire shapes, not vibes. Schema-first:
if it changes an interface, show the proto. -->

## Alternatives considered

<!-- Each with the reason it isn't the recommendation. An alternative nobody
would ever pick doesn't count. -->

## Impact per system

| System / repo | Impact | Owner ack |
| --- | --- | --- |
| | | ☐ |

## Migration & compatibility

<!-- Wire compat, rollout order, cutover style (graduated, per the plan
quality bar), rollback story. -->

## Open questions

<!-- Numbered, each with an owner. Responses reference the number. -->

## Decision

<!-- Filled at close: what was decided, by whom, on what date — then filed as
ADR-NNN if permanent. "Accepted / Rejected / Withdrawn" + one paragraph. -->
